summary: Operator workflows must unwrap the Context.dev response envelopes before reading fields: /brand/retrieve nests under "brand", /web/styleguide nests palette/typography under "styleguide", /web/extract nests the schema result under "data", socials is a [{type,url}] array, category lives under industries.eic[].industry, address is a structured object, and styleguide font families must be pulled from typography fontFamily/fontFallbacks (not collected as raw strings, which mixes in px sizes).
reason: Reading these endpoints at the top level silently drops every brand/styleguide/extract field even though the calls return ok, so brand-brief shipped with only a raw scrape dump, competitor-map clustered everything as "unknown", schema-lab reported 0% fill on successful extractions, and asset-pack fonts listed pixel sizes. A regen must re-discover the real envelope/array shapes from the live API rather than assume flat top-level scalars and a socials map.
validated_outcome: Against live bestself.co and houseofnoa.com: brand-brief returns title/description/logo/palette/typography/normalized socials/formatted address/clean ~550-byte summary; competitor-map resolves real categories and clusters by them; asset-pack and trust-check populate fully; schema-lab reports 100% fill from the data envelope; asset-pack fonts list family names only. go test ./... passes with envelope-shape regression tests for brand-brief, competitor-map, schema-lab, and asset-pack.
